header.headline: Operator Spectrum Presentation
header.subtitle: A presentation variant should keep the same poles while emphasizing clear audience-facing waypoints.

left.title: Low-Level Runtime Control
left.body: The left pole emphasizes deterministic runtime control, predictable artifacts, and narrow kernel assumptions.

right.title: High-Level Semantic Flexibility
right.body: The right pole emphasizes broader composition, easier authoring, and looser operator specialization.

continuum.label: Control <-> Flexibility
continuum.note: Presentation variants should stay semantically identical even when the framing language changes.

band_1.title: Artifact Discipline
band_1.body: Operator choices still need to preserve artifact clarity for debugging and replay.

band_2.title: Curriculum Pressure
band_2.body: The center exists because training data usually mixes both sides of the continuum.

band_3.title: Audience Framing
band_3.body: Presentation variants explain why the same continuum matters differently to different readers.

footer.note: Continuum layouts widen the DSL without falling back to raw SVG detail.
